verses.flow: The word of YHWH that came to Zephaniah the son of Cushi, son of Gedaliah, son of Amariah, son of Hezekiah, in the days of Josiah the son of Amon, king of Judah.
verses.note: HISTORICAL: Zephaniah's lineage is traced four generations back to Hezekiah, almost certainly the king of Judah (715–686 BC), making Zephaniah of royal descent — the only prophet explicitly linked to royalty. His ministry under Josiah (640–609 BC) likely preceded or accompanied the great reform of 621 BC. HEBREW: The name Zephaniah (tsephanyah, H6846) means "YHWH has hidden" or "YHWH has treasured," a fitting name for one who announces both judgment and a hidden remnant.

verses.flow: I will utterly sweep away everything from the face of the ground, declares YHWH.
verses.note: HEBREW: The phrase rendered "utterly sweep away" translates the Hebrew asoph aseph (H622), an infinitive absolute construction intensifying the verb — literally "gathering I will gather up" — conveying total, comprehensive collection for removal. THEOLOGICAL: This opening declaration sets a cosmic scope for the chapter, echoing the de-creation language of Genesis; what YHWH spoke into existence he now threatens to unsay.

words.g: the a sea or large body of water

words.m: conjunction + verb hiphil sequential perfect 1st common sing

words.g: the ruddy i.e. a human being

verses.flow: I will sweep away man and beast; I will sweep away the birds of the heavens and the fish of the sea, and the rubble along with the wicked, and I will cut off mankind from the face of the ground, declares YHWH.
verses.note: TYPOLOGY: The reversal of the creation order — man, beast, birds, fish — directly inverts Genesis 1:20–28, presenting the Day of YHWH as an un-creation event. This pattern recurs in Jeremiah 4:23–26 and reaches its eschatological climax in Revelation 6:12–17. THEOLOGICAL: The inclusion of the fish of the sea is remarkable since no earlier judgment text (flood aside) extends to marine life; it underscores the totality of cosmic judgment.

words.g: and to stretch or spread out
words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 1st common sing
conjunction + verb hiphil sequential perfect 1st common sing

verses.flow: And I will stretch out my hand against Judah and against all the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and I will cut off from this place the remnant of Baal and the name of the idolatrous priests along with the priests —

words.m: conjunction + particle + verb niphal participle active

verses.flow: those who bow down on the rooftops to the host of the heavens, and those who bow down and swear to YHWH yet swear by Milcom,
verses.note: DIVINE_COUNCIL: "The host of the heavens" (tseva ha-shamayim, H6635/H8064) refers to the astral divine beings venerated across the ANE as members of the heavenly assembly. Worship of these beings was a direct violation of the Deuteronomy 32:8–9 ordering, in which YHWH alone is Israel's allotted divine patron. Milcom was the national deity of Ammon (1 Kgs 11:5), and swearing by him constituted allegiance-transfer to a foreign divine being.

words.m: verb qal perfect 3rd common plur + suffix

verses.flow: those who have turned back from following YHWH, and those who have not sought YHWH and have not inquired of him.
Be silent before the Lord YHWH! For the day of YHWH is near; for YHWH has prepared a sacrifice; he has consecrated his invited guests.
verses.note: HEBREW: "Be silent" translates has (H2013), an interjection calling for solemn, awe-struck stillness in the presence of divine action — used similarly in Habakkuk 2:20 and Zechariah 2:13. The image of YHWH preparing a "sacrifice" (zebach, H2077) with "consecrated guests" (qedoshim, H6918) is startling: Israel itself is the sacrifice, and the invited holy ones may refer to angelic agents of judgment. DIVINE_COUNCIL: The "consecrated guests" (qedoshim) invited to the divine sacrifice likely denotes members of the heavenly host called to execute YHWH's judicial decree, consistent with divine council warfare language throughout the prophets (cf. Isa 13:3; Jer 51:27–28).

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd masc sing
conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 1st common sing

verses.flow: And it shall come to pass on the day of the sacrifice of YHWH that I will punish the officials and the king's sons and all who clothe themselves in foreign attire.

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 1st common sing

words.g: the to fill or be full of

verses.flow: And I will punish on that day everyone who leaps over the threshold, who fill their master's house with violence and deceit.

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd masc sing

verses.flow: And on that day, declares YHWH, there shall be the sound of a cry from the Fish Gate, and a wailing from the Second Quarter, and a great crashing sound from the hills.
Wail, O inhabitants of the Mortar! For all the traders are no more; all who weigh out silver are cut off.

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd masc sing
conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 1st common sing

verses.flow: And it shall come to pass at that time that I will search Jerusalem with lamps, and I will punish the men who are thickening on their dregs, who say in their hearts, "YHWH will not do good, nor will he do evil."
verses.note: HEBREW: "Thickening on their dregs" (qopheim al-shemareyhem, H7087/H8105) is a vivid winemaking metaphor: wine left undisturbed on its sediment grows thick, syrupy, and corrupted. The image depicts complacent, stagnant souls settled into spiritual inertia. THEOLOGICAL: Their functional deism — "YHWH will not do good, nor will he do evil" — is not atheism but practical denial of divine providence and governance, a theological posture the prophets consistently condemn (cf. Mal 2:17).

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd masc sing
conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur
conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur

verses.flow: And their wealth shall become plunder and their houses a desolation; and they shall build houses but not inhabit them, and they shall plant vineyards but not drink their wine.
The great day of YHWH is near, near and hastening fast; the sound of the day of YHWH — bitter is the warrior's cry there.
verses.note: HEBREW: The phrase "hastening fast" (maher meod, H4116) communicates urgent velocity; the Day is not merely approaching but racing. The Hebrew continues with a rare, vivid line: "the sound of the day of YHWH — bitter (mar) is the warrior's cry there," personifying the Day as a sound, a voice, a battle shout. STRUCTURE: This verse functions as the hinge of the chapter's central poem (vv. 14–18), transitioning from announcement to visceral description in a crescendo of six paired descriptors across vv. 15–16.

verses.flow: A day of wrath is that day, a day of distress and anguish, a day of ruin and devastation, a day of darkness and gloom, a day of clouds and thick darkness,
verses.note: TYPOLOGY: The six paired descriptors of the Day — wrath, distress/anguish, ruin/devastation, darkness/gloom, clouds/thick darkness — echo Joel 2:2 and directly inform New Testament apocalyptic, including Jesus's Olivet Discourse (Matt 24:29) and the Pauline "day of wrath" (Rom 2:5). The cumulative rhetorical weight is deliberate: no single image suffices to capture the totality of divine judgment. THEOLOGICAL: "Thick darkness" (araphel, H6205) is consistently associated in the OT with theophanic divine presence — paradoxically, YHWH's coming is simultaneously luminous terror and consuming darkness (cf. Exod 20:21; 1 Kgs 8:12).

words.g: the + in/with a city in the widest sense

verses.flow: a day of trumpet and battle-cry against the fortified cities and against the lofty battlements.

words.g: blood of man or an animal

verses.flow: And I will bring distress upon men, and they shall walk like the blind, because they have sinned against YHWH; and their blood shall be poured out like dust, and their flesh like dung.

words.m: preposition + verb hiphil infinitive construct + suffix

verses.flow: Neither their silver nor their gold shall be able to deliver them on the day of the wrath of YHWH; and in the fire of his jealousy all the earth shall be consumed, for a full, yes, a sudden end he will make of all the inhabitants of the earth.
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: The chapter closes with the phrase "in the fire of his jealousy" (be-esh qin'ato, H784/H7068), a covenantal term: YHWH's jealousy (qin'ah) is the passionate, exclusive claim of a covenant suzerain over his people, provoked by their allegiance to rival divine beings. TYPOLOGY: The language of consuming fire and sudden, total end anticipates both the Day of the Lord in 2 Peter 3:10–12 and the eschatological judgment of Revelation 19:11–21, where the Messiah executes the final divine council verdict upon the nations.

words.g: and + in/with to forage for straw
words.m: conjunction + verb qal imperative 2nd masc plur

verses.flow: Gather yourselves together, yes, gather together, O nation without shame,
verses.note: HEBREW: The opening imperative "hithqosheshu w'qoshu" (gather and gather yourselves) uses a rare Hithpael reflexive form from qashas, meaning to collect oneself or assemble. The phrase "lo nikhsaph" (without shame/longing) is debated — it may mean a nation that feels no shame or a nation not yet desired/called. The repetition creates an urgent rhetorical summons before impending judgment.

verses.flow: before the decree brings forth — like chaff the day passes away — before there comes upon you the burning anger ('aph) of YHWH, before there comes upon you the day of the wrath ('ebrah) of YHWH.
Seek YHWH, all you meek ('anawim) of the earth, who have carried out his just decree; seek righteousness (tsedaqah), seek meekness — perhaps you will be hidden in the day of the anger ('aph) of YHWH.
verses.note: HEBREW: The term 'anawim (meek/afflicted, Strong's H6041) carries covenantal weight, referring to the lowly remnant who depend wholly on YHWH rather than earthly power. "Perhaps you will be hidden" uses the verb satar (to conceal), the same root underlying the divine name El-Mistatter. TYPOLOGY: This remnant theology anticipates the Beatitudes (Matt 5:3-5), where Jesus pronounces blessing on the poor in spirit and the meek — the true 'anawim who inherit the earth.

words.g: to drive out from a possession
words.m: verb piel imperfect 3rd masc plur + suffix

verses.flow: For Gaza shall be forsaken, and Ashkelon a desolation; Ashdod, at noon they shall drive her out, and Ekron shall be uprooted.

verses.flow: Woe to the inhabitants of the seacoast, the nation of the Cherethites! The word of YHWH is against you, O Canaan, land of the Philistines — and I will destroy you until no inhabitant remains.

verses.flow: And the seacoast shall become pastures, meadows of shepherds and folds for flocks.

verses.flow: And the coast shall be for the remnant of the house of Judah; upon them they shall pasture; in the houses of Ashkelon at evening they shall lie down, for YHWH their God will attend to them and will restore their captivity.
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: The phrase "YHWH their God will attend to them" uses paqad — a covenantal visitation word implying both judgment and redemptive intervention. The promise to "restore their captivity" (shub shebut) is a classic prophetic restoration formula, signaling eschatological reversal. This verse grounds the judgment oracles of the chapter in YHWH's ultimate fidelity to his covenant people.

words.m: conjunction + verb hiphil sequential imperfect 3rd masc plur

verses.flow: I have heard the taunting of Moab and the revilings of the sons of Ammon, how they have taunted my people and made boasts against their territory.

words.m: verb qal imperfect 3rd masc plur + suffix
verb qal imperfect 3rd masc plur + suffix

verses.flow: Therefore, as I live — declares (ne'um) YHWH of hosts (tseba'oth), the God of Israel — surely Moab shall become like Sodom, and the sons of Ammon like Gomorrah: a possession of nettles, and a pit of salt, and a desolation forever. The remnant of my people shall plunder them, and the survivors of my nation shall possess them.
verses.note: HISTORICAL: The invocation of Sodom and Gomorrah as the paradigm of total divine destruction (Gen 19) marks Moab and Ammon's fate as a recapitulation of primordial judgment. Significantly, Moab and Ammon were themselves descended from Lot, the sole survivor of Sodom, making this a deeply ironic reversal. THEOLOGICAL: The phrase "as I live — declares YHWH" (hay-ani ne'um YHWH) is the divine oath formula, invoking YHWH's own existence as the guarantor of the pronouncement.

words.m: conjunction + verb hiphil sequential imperfect 3rd masc plur

verses.flow: This shall be their lot in return for their pride, because they taunted and boasted against the people of YHWH of hosts.

words.m: conjunction + verb hishtaphel imperfect 3rd masc plur

verses.flow: YHWH will be awesome against them, for he will make lean all the gods ('elohim) of the earth; and to him shall bow down, each one from his place, all the coastlands of the nations.
verses.note: DIVINE_COUNCIL: The declaration that YHWH "will make lean" (razah, to emaciate or reduce) all the gods ('elohim) of the earth is a direct polemic against the divine council members assigned to the nations (cf. Deut 32:8-9; Ps 82). These are not mere idols of wood and stone but are treated as real spiritual entities whose power YHWH will diminish and destroy. This universal subjugation of the nations' patron deities anticipates the cosmic Christus Victor theme of Col 2:15 and Phil 2:9-11.

verses.flow: You also, O Cushites — they shall be slain by my sword.

words.g: and to stretch or spread out
words.m: conjunction + verb qal jussive 3rd masc sing
conjunction + verb piel imperfect 3rd masc sing
conjunction + verb qal jussive 3rd masc sing

verses.flow: And he will stretch out his hand against the north and destroy Assyria, and he will make Nineveh a desolation, a dry waste like the desert.
verses.note: HISTORICAL: The oracle against Nineveh, capital of the Assyrian empire, was delivered when Assyria was still a dominant world power. The city fell to the Babylonian-Median coalition in 612 B.C., fulfilling this prophecy with remarkable precision. Nahum prophesied the same destruction contemporaneously. TYPOLOGY: Nineveh's pride and sudden desolation (v. 15) serves as a type of Babylon in Revelation 18, where the same boast — "I sit as queen, and I am no widow" — meets a similar catastrophic end.

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur

verses.flow: Herds shall lie down in her midst, every beast of every nation; both the pelican and the hedgehog shall lodge in the capitals of her columns; a voice shall hoot in the window; desolation shall be on the threshold, for her cedar paneling he has stripped bare.

words.g: the + in/with a city in the widest sense

verses.flow: This is the exultant city that dwelt in security, that said in her heart, "I am, and there is none besides me." How she has become a desolation, a lair for wild beasts! Everyone who passes by her hisses and shakes his fist.
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: The taunt "I am, and there is none besides me" ('ani we'ephsi 'od) is the language of exclusive divinity — the precise phrasing YHWH uses of himself in Isaiah 45:5-6. Nineveh's appropriation of divine prerogatives constitutes the height of hubris and idolatrous self-deification. The hissing and shaking of the fist by passersby are ancient ANE gestures of contempt and mourning over a fallen city, paralleled in Lamentations 2:15.

words.g: the + in/with a city in the widest sense
the to rage or be violent

verses.flow: Woe to her who is rebellious and defiled, to the oppressing city!
verses.note: HEBREW: The opening "hoy" (woe/alas; Strong's H1945) is a funeral lament particle, typically introducing oracles of doom. The city is described as "mora'ah" (rebellious, H4805) and "nig'alah" (defiled, polluted, H1351) — terms evoking ritual and moral corruption simultaneously. The identity of the city is Jerusalem, a striking reversal after oracles against the nations in chapters 1-2.

verses.flow: She has not hearkened to the voice; she has not received correction (musar); in YHWH she has not trusted; to her God she has not drawn near.
Her princes in her midst are roaring lions; her judges are wolves of the evening — they leave nothing for the morning.
Her prophets are reckless, treacherous men; her priests have profaned the holy (qodesh) things; they have done violence to the Torah.
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: The indictment against prophets and priests as the primary agents of Torah-violence is a recurring covenantal theme (Jer 2:8; Ezek 22:26). The phrase "chamesu Torah" (they have done violence to the Torah) uses the same verb for violent injustice applied to social oppression, equating spiritual corruption with civic brutality. This sets the stage for the purified leadership of the restored remnant.

words.g: and to arrange as a battle

verses.flow: YHWH is righteous within her; he does no injustice; morning by morning he renders his judgment, at the light — he does not fail — yet the unjust one knows no shame.

words.g: in/with to parch i.e. to desolate
in/with a city in the widest sense

verses.flow: I have cut off nations; their corner towers are desolate; I have laid waste their streets so that none passes through; their cities are made desolate, without a man, without an inhabitant.

I said, Surely you will fear me; you will receive correction (musar) — and her dwelling will not be cut off, all that I have appointed against her. But they rose early; they acted corruptly in all their deeds.

words.g: to/for to gather for any purpose

words.m: preposition + verb qal infinitive construct + suffix

verses.flow: Therefore wait for me, declares YHWH, for the day I rise up as a witness; for my decision is to gather nations, to assemble kingdoms, to pour out upon them my indignation, all the heat of my wrath (aph); for by the fire of my jealousy all the earth shall be consumed.
verses.note: DIVINE_COUNCIL: The language of YHWH rising as a witness ("le'ed," H5707) and assembling the nations for judgment evokes the divine council courtroom scene (Ps 82:1; Deut 32:8-9). The outpouring of wrath upon assembled kingdoms carries cosmic-judicial overtones — YHWH as sovereign judge executing sentence upon nations under corrupt divine administration. TYPOLOGY: The eschatological gathering for judgment prefigures the final assize (Rev 16:14; Matt 25:32).

words.m: preposition + verb qal infinitive construct + suffix

verses.flow: For at that time I will transform the peoples to a pure language (saphah berurah), that all of them may call upon the name of YHWH, to serve him with one shoulder.
verses.note: HEBREW: "Saphah berurah" — literally "a pure/clean lip." Saphah (H8193) means lip or language; berurah (H1305) means purified, refined. This is a direct reversal of the Babel event (Gen 11:1-9) where humanity's language was confused. THEOLOGICAL: The restoration of a pure tongue enabling unified worship anticipates Pentecost (Acts 2) and the eschatological gathering of all nations to call upon YHWH's name (Rom 10:13; Rev 7:9-10).

words.m: verb hiphil imperfect 3rd masc plur + suffix

verses.flow: From beyond the rivers of Cush my suppliants — the daughter of my dispersed ones — shall bring my offering.

words.g: in/with a mountain or range of hills

verses.flow: On that day you shall not be put to shame on account of all your deeds by which you have rebelled against me; for then I will remove from your midst the ones who exult in your pride, and you shall no longer be haughty in my holy mountain.

words.m: conjunction + verb hiphil sequential perfect 1st common sing
conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur

verses.flow: But I will leave in your midst a people humble and lowly, and they shall take refuge in the name of YHWH.
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: The preserved remnant is characterized by "ani" (humble, H6041) and "dal" (lowly, H1800) — terms describing economic and social marginalization as well as spiritual dependence. This inverts worldly standards of power and anticipates the beatitudes (Matt 5:3-5). The remnant's defining trait is refuge in YHWH's name, not human strength or institutional prestige.

words.m: conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 3rd common plur

verses.flow: The remnant of Israel shall do no injustice and speak no lie, and in their mouth shall not be found a deceitful tongue; for they shall graze and lie down, and there shall be none to make them afraid.
verses.note: TYPOLOGY: The portrait of the remnant — no injustice, no falsehood, no deceit — directly prefigures the servant community of Isaiah 53:9 and is cited in Revelation 14:5 regarding the 144,000 who follow the Lamb. The pastoral image of grazing and lying down without fear echoes the Good Shepherd motif (Ps 23; John 10:9-11), pointing to Messiah's reign.

words.m: conjunction + verb qal imperative 2nd fem sing

verses.flow: Shout aloud, O daughter of Zion! Shout for joy, O Israel! Rejoice and exult with all your heart, O daughter of Jerusalem!
YHWH has removed your judgments; he has turned away your enemy. The King of Israel, YHWH, is in your midst; you shall not fear evil anymore.
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: The declaration "Melek Yisrael YHWH" — "the King of Israel, YHWH" — is a stunning royal affirmation. YHWH's kingship in the midst of Israel fulfills the ancient theocratic ideal (Ex 15:18; Num 23:21). TYPOLOGY: The NT identifies Jesus as this divine King present "in the midst" of his people (Matt 18:20; John 1:14; Rev 21:3), making this verse a significant Christological node.

verses.flow: On that day it shall be said to Jerusalem: Do not fear, O Zion; let not your hands fall limp.
YHWH your God is in your midst, a mighty one (gibbor) who will save; he will rejoice over you with gladness (simchah); he will be quiet in his love (ahavah); he will exult over you with loud singing.
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: Zephaniah 3:17 is one of the most dense restoration affirmations in the Hebrew canon. "Gibbor" (H1368, mighty warrior/champion) applied to YHWH in the midst of his people echoes Isaiah 9:6 where "El Gibbor" is a title of the messianic child-king. The verb "yachrish" (he will be quiet/silent) in love is debated — possibly indicating love so profound it transcends expression. HEBREW: "Yasis alayikh berinah" — he will spin/leap for joy over you with a ringing cry — employs the most exuberant vocabulary of celebration in the OT, applied remarkably to YHWH's own emotional response to his restored people.

verses.flow: I will gather those who are grieved over the appointed feast (moed), who were from you — the burden of reproach upon her was theirs.

words.m: conjunction + verb hiphil sequential perfect 1st common sing
conjunction + particle + verb niphal participle passive
conjunction + verb qal sequential perfect 1st common sing + suffix

verses.flow: Behold, at that time I will deal with all your oppressors; and I will save the lame, and the outcast I will gather; and I will make them a praise and a name in every land of their shame.
verses.note: TYPOLOGY: The gathering of the lame and the outcast mirrors Jesus's healings and table fellowship with marginalized persons (Luke 14:21; Matt 15:31), enacted demonstrations of the eschatological restoration Zephaniah envisions. The reversal of shame into praise and renown anticipates the honor bestowed upon the redeemed before all nations (Isa 62:7; Rev 21:26).

words.m: preposition + verb qal infinitive construct + suffix

verses.flow: At that time I will bring you in, at the time I gather you together; for I will make you a name and a praise among all the peoples of the earth, when I restore your fortunes before your eyes, says YHWH.
verses.note: THEOLOGICAL: The book closes with YHWH's own first-person pledge: "I will restore your fortunes" (shavti et shevutkem) — the covenant restoration formula found throughout the prophets (Jer 29:14; 30:3; Amos 9:14). The phrase "before your eyes" (le'eyneykhem) emphasizes the visible, historical, and publicly witnessed nature of this restoration, not merely a spiritual metaphor. This final promise anchors Zephaniah's cosmic Day of YHWH not in annihilation but in renewal.
